reviews.author: maimoona lateef
reviews.review_body:
Dear Sheikh Bushra Ahmed,

Be thankful to the principal and teachers for being kind towards you. In spite of your every day's baseless complaints school tolerated you in every way .
Inculcate the habit of being thankful as it will help you in being social in the society. Else you'll have to move from school to school as you did in the last year.

reviews.author: Pooja Magar
reviews.review_body:
Dear Sheik Bushra Ahmed,

Let me remind you this school has given 100%support to you in every way. Though you didn't pay any fee since the admission was done,Principal of the school didn't stop your child from coming to school.In fact your child was given lots of attention like any other student. Where as mentioned teachers name in the list,are the best teachers of the school. They tolerated your every day's complaints and your arguments with them. Hatsoff to those teacher's patience who has the ability to bear such parents who are so thank less.
A sincere advice to you .
Whether it is Rainbow hospital or school you  should be thankful for their services as they are providing you all benefits free of cost. Rather than being thankful you are being too unacknowledged.
Such derogatory comments on an educational school and teachers will lead to defamation and you can face legal consequences.

reviews.author: Sheikh Bushra Ahmed
reviews.review_body:
I am writing to express my deep concern and disappointment with the treatment my child has received at  LOVEDALE INTERNATIONAL SCHOOL in Hyderabad.

My 6-year-old son was physically abused by his kindergarten facilitator, Ms. Harita, and her assistant, Ms. Naseem. They beat him multiple times for playing with an ID card and not writing quickly enough. This incident has left my child traumatized and fearful of attending school.

The school's infrastructure, despite its promise, falls short in terms of maintenance and cleanliness. Classrooms and washrooms are often dirty and poorly maintained.

Furthermore, my child was repeatedly subjected to unfair treatment by teachers. Despite my requests, they insisted on seating my son at the back of the classroom, where mosquitoes and insects were prevalent. This was not only uncomfortable but also posed health risks.

It's alarming that teachers would intentionally disregard a parent's concerns, seemingly due to personal biases and anger issues. This behavior is unacceptable and creates a hostile learning environment.

These experiences have led me to question the school's commitment to providing a safe, healthy, and supportive environment for its students.

Furthermore, despite having a prior agreement with the administration to pay the outstanding fees by the end of the month, my child was not allowed to sit for the 2nd-term exams. The teachers humiliated him by asking him to put his head down and sit quietly until dismissal.

I subsequently spoke with the Principal, who shockingly defended the school's actions, citing "protocol" as the reason. I was appalled to hear this response from someone in a position of authority and responsibility.

It has become clear that the school prioritizes business interests over the well-being and education of its students. This is unacceptable and has led to a toxic environment that harms children.

I am extremely dissatisfied with the school's actions and will not recommend it to any parents. The school's bullying behavior towards students of all ages is unacceptable and has left a lasting impact on my child. I hope that in the future, the school will take concrete steps to address these issues and create a safe and supportive learning environment.

reviews.author: QURRAT UL AIEN Aieny
reviews.review_body:
Very Small area!! no campus
no ground good for pre primary schooling who live in surrounding for Small kids not bad option but for primary is like a prison

reviews.author: Attu Home
reviews.review_body:
Dont join this school. Its the worst school ever. The grand kids of principal bully all the students of the school. They are given more priorities.Teachers dont pay attention to other students they give more priority to grand kids of principal. Till now PTM is not conducted for PT2 We dont know our child's performance. I will never recommend this school to anyone.
They dont follow any government rules and this school is not affiliated to cbse.

reviews.author: Mohammed
reviews.review_body: I don't understand why this school is closed on November 14th as it's  Children's Day. I checked every other school is having activities or programs for the day. I remember that in my school, we used to have special events and parties for the kids to celebrate. It's surprising that this school has decided to make it a non-working day. They don't want to spend anything on activities or functions for the kids' entertainment, it seems like everything has become commercialized I'm really disappointed.

reviews.author: SHAIFA MOIZA
reviews.review_body:
Hi, first of all, sorry for writing the review, but this is the truth about what's happening in the school.
They provide an events calendar, but it is not followed.
There are no school trips they said there is one but nothing.
There are no extra activities for classes 9 and 10, despite their claim that classes 9 and 10 are allowed for the first term.
The computer classes for classes 2 to 8 are not useful as they are still teaching Windows 7 in this modern era.
The projectors in the class won't work even after requesting to get them ready.
The boys' restroom is not clean and not closing properly.
The management seems to be unaware of everything.
In summary, I have never seen a school like this.
They seem to give more importance to the principal's grandchildren in everything, as if it's their school and they can do whatever they want.

reviews.author: SMART HUNK
reviews.review_body: Commendable efforts by the principal and staff in grooming students. School targets individual attention.

reviews.author: Samida Lalani
reviews.review_body: Mam, I'm sorry to say that first of all you do not know what syllabus the school is following. And you are targeting the school for your personal issues. Afreen Banu is a responsible person and is doing her job efficiently. You cannot tarnish anyone's name for your personal grudges.
